includeGridData.description: True if grid data should be returned. Ignored if a field mask is set. Please provide a value of type boolean.
includeGridData.title: Includegriddata

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to request. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheetid

value.description: Returns the spreadsheet at the given ID, filtered by the specified data filters. Use this tool when you need to retrieve specific subsets of data from a Google Sheet based on criteria like A1 notation, developer metadata, or grid ranges. Important: This action is designed for filtered data retrieval. While it accepts empty filters and returns full metadata in that case, GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO is the recommended action for unfiltered spreadsheet retrieval.
value.display_description: Returns the spreadsheet at the given ID, filtered by the specified data filters. Use this tool when you need to retrieve specific subsets of data from a Google Sheet based on criteria like A1 notation, developer metadata, or grid ranges. Important: This action is designed for filtered data retrieval. While it accepts empty filters and returns full metadata in that case, GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO is the recommended action for unfiltered spreadsheet retrieval.
value.name: googlesheets_get_spreadsheet_by_data_filter

exclude_tables_in_banded_ranges.description: Optional. If true, tables within banded ranges will be omitted from the response. Default is false when not specified. Please provide a value of type boolean.
exclude_tables_in_banded_ranges.title: Exclude Tables In Banded Ranges

fields.description: Optional. Field mask specifying which fields to return. Uses Google's field mask syntax (comma-separated, dot-notation for nested fields). If not specified, a default mask returning common fields (spreadsheet ID, title, sheet properties) is applied to avoid payload size issues. For full metadata, use '*' (not recommended for large spreadsheets). When set, includeGridData is ignored. Examples: 'sheets.properties(sheetId,title)', 'properties.title,sheets.properties.sheetId'. Please provide a value of type string.
fields.title: Fields

include_grid_data.description: Optional. If true, grid data will be returned. This parameter is ignored if a field mask was set in the request. When false or not specified, only metadata is returned without cell values. Please provide a value of type boolean.
include_grid_data.title: Include Grid Data

ranges.description: Optional. The ranges to retrieve from the spreadsheet, specified using A1 notation (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:D5', 'Sheet2!A1:C4'). Multiple ranges can be requested simultaneously. If not specified, metadata for the entire spreadsheet is returned without grid data.
ranges.title: Ranges

spreadsheet_id.description: Required. The Google Sheets spreadsheet ID or full URL. Accepts either the ID alone (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms') or a full Google Sheets URL (e.g., 'https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms/edit'). The ID will be automatically extracted from URLs. Note: Published/embedded URLs (containing '/d/e/2PACX-...') are not supported. Please provide a value of type string.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

value.description: Retrieves metadata for a Google Spreadsheet using its ID. By default, returns essential information (ID, title, sheet properties) to avoid payload size issues. Use the fields parameter for comprehensive metadata or specific fields.
value.display_description: Retrieves metadata for a Google Spreadsheet using its ID. By default, returns essential information (ID, title, sheet properties) to avoid payload size issues. Use the fields parameter for comprehensive metadata or specific fields.
value.name: googlesheets_get_spreadsheet_info

sample_size.description: Number of rows to sample for type inference. Please provide a value of type integer.
sample_size.title: Sample Size

sheet_name.description: Sheet/tab name if table_name is ambiguous across multiple sheets. Please provide a value of type string.
sheet_name.title: Sheet Name

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet. Must be a valid Google Sheets ID (typically a 44-character alphanumeric string). Do NOT use 'auto' - only 'table_name' supports auto-detection. You can get this ID from the spreadsheet URL or from SEARCH_SPREADSHEETS action. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

table_name.description: Table name from LIST_TABLES response OR the visible Google Sheets tab name (e.g., 'Sales Data', 'Projections'). Use 'auto' to analyze the largest/most prominent table. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
table_name.title: Table Name

value.description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SHEET_NAMES and GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO for sheet structure metadata, and GOOGLESHEETS_VALUES_GET for direct range inspection. This action is used to get the schema of a table in a Google Spreadsheet, call this action to get the schema of a table in a spreadsheet BEFORE YOU QUERY THE TABLE. Analyze table structure and infer column names, types, and constraints. Uses statistical analysis of sample data to determine the most likely data type for each column. Call this action after calling the LIST_TABLES action to get the schema of a table in a spreadsheet.
value.display_description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SHEET_NAMES and GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO for sheet structure metadata, and GOOGLESHEETS_VALUES_GET for direct range inspection. This action is used to get the schema of a table in a Google Spreadsheet, call this action to get the schema of a table in a spreadsheet BEFORE YOU QUERY THE TABLE. Analyze table structure and infer column names, types, and constraints. Uses statistical analysis of sample data to determine the most likely data type for each column. Call this action after calling the LIST_TABLES action to get the schema of a table in a spreadsheet.
value.name: googlesheets_get_table_schema

include_spreadsheet_in_response.description: True if the updated spreadsheet should be included in the response. Please provide a value of type boolean.
include_spreadsheet_in_response.title: Include Spreadsheet In Response

insert_dimension.description: The details for the insert dimension request. This parameter is required.

response_include_grid_data.description: True if grid data should be included in the response (if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true). Please provide a value of type boolean.
response_include_grid_data.title: Response Include Grid Data

response_ranges.description: Limits the ranges of the spreadsheet to include in the response.
response_ranges.title: Response Ranges

spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

value.description: Tool to insert new rows or columns into a sheet at a specified location. Use when you need to add empty rows or columns within an existing Google Sheet.
value.display_description: Tool to insert new rows or columns into a sheet at a specified location. Use when you need to add empty rows or columns within an existing Google Sheet.
value.name: googlesheets_insert_dimension

min_columns.description: Minimum number of columns to consider a valid table. Please provide a value of type integer.
min_columns.title: Min Columns

min_confidence.description: Minimum confidence score (0.0-1.0) to consider a valid table. Please provide a value of type number.
min_confidence.title: Min Confidence

min_rows.description: Minimum number of data rows to consider a valid table. Please provide a value of type integer.
min_rows.title: Min Rows

spreadsheet_id.description: The actual Google Spreadsheet ID (not a placeholder or spreadsheet name). Find it in the spreadsheet URL: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/{SPREADSHEET_ID}/edit. It is the alphanumeric string between '/d/' and '/edit' (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'). IMPORTANT: Do NOT pass the spreadsheet name - only pass the alphanumeric ID from the URL. Do NOT pass template placeholders like '{{spreadsheet_id}}', '<spreadsheet_id>', or 'your-spreadsheet-id-here'. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

value.description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SHEET_NAMES for tab discovery and GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO for full sheet metadata. This action is used to list all tables in a Google Spreadsheet, call this action to get the list of tables in a spreadsheet. Discover all tables in a Google Spreadsheet by analyzing sheet structure and detecting data patterns. Uses heuristic analysis to find header rows, data boundaries, and table structures.
value.display_description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SHEET_NAMES for tab discovery and GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO for full sheet metadata. This action is used to list all tables in a Google Spreadsheet, call this action to get the list of tables in a spreadsheet. Discover all tables in a Google Spreadsheet by analyzing sheet structure and detecting data patterns. Uses heuristic analysis to find header rows, data boundaries, and table structures.
value.name: googlesheets_list_tables

case_sensitive.description: If `True`, the query string search is case-sensitive. Please provide a value of type boolean.
case_sensitive.title: Case Sensitive

date_time_render_option.description: How dates and times are represented. FORMATTED_STRING: human-readable strings (e.g. '2025-12-18 11:17'). SERIAL_NUMBER: Excel-style serial numbers. Works with all value_render_option settings. Please provide a value of type string.
date_time_render_option.title: Date Time Render Option

normalize_whitespace.description: If `True`, strips leading and trailing whitespace from cell values before matching. This helps match cells like ' TOTAL ' or 'TOTAL ' when searching for 'TOTAL'. Please provide a value of type boolean.
normalize_whitespace.title: Normalize Whitespace

query.description: Exact text value to find; matches the entire content of a cell in a row. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
query.title: Query

range.description: A1 notation range to search within. Supports cell ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:D5'), column-only ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1!A:Z'), and row-only ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1!1:1'). Defaults to the first sheet if omitted. IMPORTANT: Sheet names with spaces must be single-quoted (e.g., "'My Sheet'!A1:Z"). Bare sheet names without ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1') are not supported - always specify a range. Please provide a value of type string.
range.title: Range

spreadsheet_id.description: Identifier of the Google Spreadsheet to search. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

value_render_option.description: How cell values are rendered in the returned row data. unformatted: raw values without display formatting — dates appear as serial numbers, e.g. 46009.47 (default, keeps consistency with UPSERT). formatted: display-formatted values — dates appear as strings, e.g. '2025-12-18'. formula: raw formulas instead of computed values. Please provide a value of type string.
value_render_option.title: Value Render Option

value.description: Finds the first row in a Google Spreadsheet where a cell's entire content exactly matches the query string, searching within a specified A1 notation range or the first sheet by default.
value.display_description: Finds the first row in a Google Spreadsheet where a cell's entire content exactly matches the query string, searching within a specified A1 notation range or the first sheet by default.
value.name: googlesheets_lookup_spreadsheet_row

index.description: Zero-based index for the operation. Required for UPDATE, DELETE, MOVE. Optional for ADD (defaults to end of list). Please provide a value of type integer.
index.title: Index

new_index.description: Destination index for MOVE operation. Required when operation is MOVE. Please provide a value of type integer.
new_index.title: New Index

operation.description: Operation type: ADD (add new rule), UPDATE (replace rule), DELETE (remove rule), MOVE (change rule order/index). Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
operation.title: Operation

sheet_id.description: The unique numeric identifier of the sheet/tab to modify (NOT a zero-based index). This is a specific ID assigned by Google Sheets when the sheet is created, not the position of the sheet. You MUST first call GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO to retrieve the actual sheetId values from the 'sheets' array in the response. Common mistake: Do not assume sheet_id=0 exists - while some spreadsheets may have a sheet with ID 0, many do not. Please provide a value of type integer. This parameter is required.
sheet_id.title: Sheet Id

spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet containing the sheet to modify. Found in the Google Sheets URL between '/d/' and '/edit'. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

value.description: Add, update, delete, or reorder conditional format rules on a Google Sheet. Use when you need to create, modify, or remove conditional formatting without manually building batchUpdate requests. Supports four operations: ADD (create new rule), UPDATE (replace existing rule), DELETE (remove rule), MOVE (reorder rules by changing index).
value.display_description: Add, update, delete, or reorder conditional format rules on a Google Sheet. Use when you need to create, modify, or remove conditional formatting without manually building batchUpdate requests. Supports four operations: ADD (create new rule), UPDATE (replace existing rule), DELETE (remove rule), MOVE (reorder rules by changing index).
value.name: googlesheets_mutate_conditional_format_rules

include_formulas.description: Whether to return formula text instead of calculated values for formula columns. Please provide a value of type boolean.
include_formulas.title: Include Formulas

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of a native Google Sheets file. Found in the spreadsheet URL after /d/ (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'). Only native Google Sheets files (MIME type: application/vnd.google-apps.spreadsheet) are supported. Files uploaded to Google Drive that are not native Google Sheets (such as Excel .xlsx files, PDFs, or Google Docs) will not work even if they can be viewed in Google Sheets. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

sql.description: SQL SELECT query. The table name is the Google Sheets tab/sheet name (visible at the bottom of the spreadsheet). Use GOOGLESHEETS_LIST_TABLES to discover available table names if unknown. Supported: SELECT cols FROM table WHERE conditions ORDER BY col LIMIT n. Table names must be quoted with double quotes if they contain spaces or are numeric-only (e.g., SELECT * FROM "My Sheet" or SELECT * FROM "415"). Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
examples: SELECT project, totals FROM "Sales_Data" WHERE totals > 10.0 ORDER BY totals DESC
SELECT name, email FROM "Customers" WHERE status = 'ACTIVE'
sql.title: Sql

value.description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_VALUES_GET / GOOGLESHEETS_BATCH_GET for table reads and GOOGLESHEETS_LOOKUP_SPREADSHEET_ROW for row lookup/filter workflows. Execute SQL-like SELECT queries against Google Spreadsheet tables. Table names correspond to sheet/tab names visible at the bottom of the spreadsheet. Use GOOGLESHEETS_LIST_TABLES first to discover available table names if unknown. Supports WHERE conditions, ORDER BY, LIMIT clauses.
value.display_description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_VALUES_GET / GOOGLESHEETS_BATCH_GET for table reads and GOOGLESHEETS_LOOKUP_SPREADSHEET_ROW for row lookup/filter workflows. Execute SQL-like SELECT queries against Google Spreadsheet tables. Table names correspond to sheet/tab names visible at the bottom of the spreadsheet. Use GOOGLESHEETS_LIST_TABLES first to discover available table names if unknown. Supports WHERE conditions, ORDER BY, LIMIT clauses.
value.name: googlesheets_query_table

dataFilters.description: The data filters describing the criteria used to determine which DeveloperMetadata entries to return. This parameter is required.
dataFilters.title: Datafilters

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve metadata from. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheetid

value.description: Tool to search for developer metadata in a spreadsheet. Use when you need to find specific metadata entries based on filters.
value.display_description: Tool to search for developer metadata in a spreadsheet. Use when you need to find specific metadata entries based on filters.
value.name: googlesheets_search_developer_metadata

created_after.description: Return spreadsheets created after this date. Use RFC 3339 format like '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'. Please provide a value of type string.
created_after.title: Created After

include_shared_drives.description: Whether to include spreadsheets from shared drives you have access to. Defaults to True. Please provide a value of type boolean.
include_shared_drives.title: Include Shared Drives

include_trashed.description: Whether to include spreadsheets in trash. Defaults to False. Please provide a value of type boolean.
include_trashed.title: Include Trashed

max_results.description: Maximum number of spreadsheets to return (1-1000). Defaults to 10. Please provide a value of type integer.
max_results.title: Max Results

modified_after.description: Return spreadsheets modified after this date. Use RFC 3339 format like '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'. Please provide a value of type string.
modified_after.title: Modified After

order_by.description: Sort order (comma-separated list for multi-field sorting). Valid fields: createdTime, folder, modifiedByMeTime, modifiedTime, name, name_natural, quotaBytesUsed, recency, sharedWithMeTime, starred, viewedByMeTime. Append ' desc' for descending order (default is ascending). Examples: 'modifiedTime desc', 'folder,name', 'starred,modifiedTime desc'. Please provide a value of type string.
order_by.title: Order By

page_token.description: Token for retrieving the next page of results. Use the 'next_page_token' value from a previous response to get subsequent pages. Leave empty to get the first page. Please provide a value of type string.
page_token.title: Page Token

query.description: Search query to filter spreadsheets. Behavior depends on the 'search_type' parameter. For advanced searches, use Google Drive query syntax with fields like 'name contains', 'fullText contains', or boolean filters like 'sharedWithMe = true'. DO NOT use spreadsheet IDs as search terms. Leave empty to get all spreadsheets. Please provide a value of type string.
query.title: Query

search_type.description: How to search: 'name' searches filenames only (prefix matching from the START of filenames), 'content' uses fullText search which searches file content, name, description, and metadata (Google Drive API limitation: cannot search content exclusively without also matching filenames), 'both' explicitly searches both name OR content with an OR condition. Note: 'name' search only matches from the START of filenames (e.g., 'Budget' finds 'Budget 2024' but NOT 'Q1 Budget'). Please provide a value of type string.
search_type.title: Search Type

shared_with_me.description: Whether to return only spreadsheets shared with the current user. Defaults to False. Please provide a value of type boolean.
shared_with_me.title: Shared With Me

starred_only.description: Whether to return only starred spreadsheets. Defaults to False. Please provide a value of type boolean.
starred_only.title: Starred Only

value.description: Search for Google Spreadsheets using various filters including name, content, date ranges, and more.
value.display_description: Search for Google Spreadsheets using various filters including name, content, date ranges, and more.
value.name: googlesheets_search_spreadsheets

filter.description: The filter to set. This parameter is required.

includeSpreadsheetInResponse.description: Determines if the updated spreadsheet resource appears in the response. Default is false. Please provide a value of type boolean.
includeSpreadsheetInResponse.title: Includespreadsheetinresponse

responseIncludeGridData.description: True if grid data should be returned. Meaningful only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. Ignored if a field mask was set in the request. Please provide a value of type boolean.
responseIncludeGridData.title: Responseincludegriddata

responseRanges.description: Limits the ranges included in the response spreadsheet. Meaningful only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true.
responseRanges.title: Responseranges

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheetid

value.description: Tool to set a basic filter on a sheet in a Google Spreadsheet. Use when you need to filter or sort data within a specific range on a sheet.
value.display_description: Tool to set a basic filter on a sheet in a Google Spreadsheet. Use when you need to filter or sort data within a specific range on a sheet.
value.name: googlesheets_set_basic_filter

condition_values.description: Generic list of condition values for validation types that require specific values (e.g., NUMBER_GREATER requires one value, NUMBER_BETWEEN requires two values, TEXT_CONTAINS requires one value). For simple validations like TEXT_IS_EMAIL, BLANK, NOT_BLANK, BOOLEAN, DATE_IS_VALID, this can be omitted. Each value should be a string that will be parsed by Google Sheets.
condition_values.title: Condition Values

end_column_index.description: Ending column index (0-based, exclusive) for the validation range. To apply to column A only, use start_column_index=0 and end_column_index=1. Please provide a value of type integer. This parameter is required.
end_column_index.title: End Column Index

end_row_index.description: Ending row index (0-based, exclusive) for the validation range. To apply to row 1 only, use start_row_index=0 and end_row_index=1. Please provide a value of type integer. This parameter is required.
end_row_index.title: End Row Index

filtered_rows_included.description: Whether to apply validation to rows hidden by filters. Default is false. Set to true to ensure validation applies to both visible and filtered rows. Please provide a value of type boolean.
filtered_rows_included.title: Filtered Rows Included

formula.description: Custom formula for validation. Required when validation_type='CUSTOM_FORMULA'. Formula should evaluate to TRUE/FALSE. Example: '=A1>10'. Please provide a value of type string.
formula.title: Formula

input_message.description: Optional message shown to the user when they select the cell. Helpful hint about what values are expected. Please provide a value of type string.
input_message.title: Input Message

mode.description: Operation mode: 'SET' applies a validation rule to the range, 'CLEAR' removes any existing validation from the range. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
mode.title: Mode

sheet_id.description: The unique sheet ID (numeric identifier) where the validation rule will be applied. The first sheet created in a spreadsheet typically has ID 0, while additional sheets get unique IDs (e.g., 1534097477). If a sheet is deleted, its ID is never reused - so if the original first sheet (ID 0) was deleted, attempting to use 0 will fail. Always verify the actual sheet ID exists using GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO action (check 'sheets[].properties.sheetId' field). Please provide a value of type integer. This parameter is required.
sheet_id.title: Sheet Id

show_custom_ui.description: Whether to show a dropdown UI for list-based validation. Default is true. Set to true for dropdown lists. Please provide a value of type boolean.
show_custom_ui.title: Show Custom Ui

source_range_a1.description: Source range in A1 notation for dropdown values. Required when validation_type='ONE_OF_RANGE'. Example: 'Sheet1!A1:A10' or 'A1:A10'. Please provide a value of type string.
source_range_a1.title: Source Range A1

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Sheets spreadsheet. Can be found in the spreadsheet URL between '/d/' and '/edit'. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

start_column_index.description: Starting column index (0-based, inclusive) for the validation range. Column A is index 0. Please provide a value of type integer. This parameter is required.
start_column_index.title: Start Column Index

start_row_index.description: Starting row index (0-based, inclusive) for the validation range. Row 1 is index 0. Please provide a value of type integer. This parameter is required.
start_row_index.title: Start Row Index

strict.description: Whether to reject invalid data (true) or show a warning (false). Default is true. Please provide a value of type boolean.
strict.title: Strict

validation_type.description: Type of validation rule to apply. Required when mode='SET'. Dropdown types: 'ONE_OF_LIST' (dropdown from list), 'ONE_OF_RANGE' (dropdown from range). Number validations: 'NUMBER_GREATER', 'NUMBER_GREATER_THAN_EQ', 'NUMBER_LESS', 'NUMBER_LESS_THAN_EQ', 'NUMBER_EQ', 'NUMBER_NOT_EQ', 'NUMBER_BETWEEN', 'NUMBER_NOT_BETWEEN'. Text validations: 'TEXT_CONTAINS', 'TEXT_NOT_CONTAINS', 'TEXT_EQ', 'TEXT_NOT_EQ', 'TEXT_IS_EMAIL', 'TEXT_IS_URL' (Note: TEXT_STARTS_WITH and TEXT_ENDS_WITH are only for conditional formatting, not data validation). Date validations: 'DATE_EQ', 'DATE_BEFORE', 'DATE_AFTER', 'DATE_ON_OR_BEFORE', 'DATE_ON_OR_AFTER', 'DATE_BETWEEN', 'DATE_NOT_BETWEEN', 'DATE_NOT_EQ', 'DATE_IS_VALID'. Other: 'BLANK', 'NOT_BLANK', 'BOOLEAN', 'CUSTOM_FORMULA'. Please provide a value of type string.
validation_type.title: Validation Type

values.description: List of allowed values for dropdown. Required when validation_type='ONE_OF_LIST'. Each item becomes a dropdown option.
values.title: Values

value.description: Tool to set or clear data validation rules (including dropdowns) on a range in Google Sheets. Use when you need to apply dropdown lists, range-based dropdowns, or custom formula validation to cells.
value.display_description: Tool to set or clear data validation rules (including dropdowns) on a range in Google Sheets. Use when you need to apply dropdown lists, range-based dropdowns, or custom formula validation to cells.
value.name: googlesheets_set_data_validation_rule

sheet_json.description: A list of dictionaries representing the rows of the sheet. Each dictionary must have the same set of keys, which will form the header row. Values can be strings, numbers, booleans, or null (represented as empty cells). An empty list [] is allowed and will create a spreadsheet with an empty worksheet. This parameter is required.
sheet_json.title: Sheet Json

sheet_name.description: The name for the first worksheet within the newly created spreadsheet. This name will appear as a tab at the bottom of the sheet. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
sheet_name.title: Sheet Name

title.description: The desired title for the new Google Spreadsheet. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
title.title: Title

value.description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_CREATE_GOOGLE_SHEET1 + GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_VALUES_BATCH (or GOOGLESHEETS_VALUES_UPDATE / GOOGLESHEETS_SPREADSHEETS_VALUES_APPEND) instead. Creates a new Google Spreadsheet and populates its first worksheet from `sheet_json`. When data is provided, the first item's keys establish the headers. An empty list creates an empty worksheet.
value.display_description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_CREATE_GOOGLE_SHEET1 + GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_VALUES_BATCH (or GOOGLESHEETS_VALUES_UPDATE / GOOGLESHEETS_SPREADSHEETS_VALUES_APPEND) instead. Creates a new Google Spreadsheet and populates its first worksheet from `sheet_json`. When data is provided, the first item's keys establish the headers. An empty list creates an empty worksheet.
value.name: googlesheets_sheet_from_json

destination_spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to copy the sheet to. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
destination_spreadsheet_id.title: Destination Spreadsheet Id

sheet_id.description: The ID of the sheet to copy. Please provide a value of type integer. This parameter is required.
sheet_id.title: Sheet Id

spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet containing the sheet to copy. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

value.description: Tool to copy a single sheet from a spreadsheet to another spreadsheet. Use when you need to duplicate a sheet into a different spreadsheet.
value.display_description: Tool to copy a single sheet from a spreadsheet to another spreadsheet. Use when you need to duplicate a sheet into a different spreadsheet.
value.name: googlesheets_spreadsheets_sheets_copy_to

includeValuesInResponse.description: Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were appended. By default, responses do not include the updated values. Please provide a value of type boolean.
includeValuesInResponse.title: Includevaluesinresponse

insertDataOption.description: How the input data should be inserted. Please provide a value of type string.
insertDataOption.title: Insertdataoption

majorDimension.description: How to interpret the 2D values array. Use ROWS for row-wise data (most common for appends). Use COLUMNS for column-wise data. Example: if A1=1,B1=2,A2=3,B2=4 then majorDimension=ROWS yields [[1,2],[3,4]] and majorDimension=COLUMNS yields [[1,3],[2,4]]. Please provide a value of type string.
majorDimension.title: Majordimension

range.description: A1 notation range used to locate a logical table. New rows are appended after the last row of that table within this range. Valid formats: sheet name only (e.g., 'Sheet1'), column range (e.g., 'Sheet1!A:D'), or cell range (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:D100'). Sheet names with spaces need single quotes (auto-added). The sheet name must exist in the spreadsheet; a non-existent sheet will cause an 'Unable to parse range' error. IMPORTANT: The append may land in different columns than specified due to API table detection. For strict column placement, use GOOGLESHEETS_SPREADSHEETS_VALUES_UPDATE instead. Always check updates.updatedRange in the response to verify where data was actually written. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
range.title: Range

responseDateTimeRenderOption.description: Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered. This is ignored if responseValueRenderOption is FORMATTED_VALUE. The default dateTime render option is SERIAL_NUMBER. Please provide a value of type string.
responseDateTimeRenderOption.title: Responsedatetimerenderoption

responseValueRenderOption.description: Determines how values in the response should be rendered. The default render option is FORMATTED_VALUE. Please provide a value of type string.
responseValueRenderOption.title: Responsevaluerenderoption

spreadsheetId.description: The spreadsheet ID (typically 44 characters containing letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores). Found in the URL between /d/ and /edit. NOT the sheet name (tab name) - that belongs in the 'range' parameter. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheetid

valueInputOption.description: How the input data should be interpreted. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
valueInputOption.title: Valueinputoption

values.description: 2D array of values to append. Typically, each inner list is a ROW (majorDimension=ROWS). Use null/None for empty cells. This parameter is required.
values.title: Values

value.description: Tool to append values to a spreadsheet. Use when you need to add new data to the end of an existing table in a Google Sheet.
value.display_description: Tool to append values to a spreadsheet. Use when you need to add new data to the end of an existing table in a Google Sheet.
value.name: googlesheets_spreadsheets_values_append

ranges.description: The ranges to clear, in A1 notation (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:B2') or R1C1 notation. Each range should be a clean string without surrounding brackets or extra quotes. Valid examples: 'Sheet1!A1:B2', 'A1:Z100', 'Sheet1'. Invalid examples: "['Sheet1!A1:B2']", '[Sheet1!A1]'. This parameter is required.
ranges.title: Ranges

spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update. Can be either a spreadsheet ID or a full Google Sheets URL (the ID will be extracted automatically). Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

value.description: Tool to clear one or more ranges of values from a spreadsheet. Use when you need to remove data from specific cells or ranges while keeping formatting and other properties intact.
value.display_description: Tool to clear one or more ranges of values from a spreadsheet. Use when you need to remove data from specific cells or ranges while keeping formatting and other properties intact.
value.name: googlesheets_spreadsheets_values_batch_clear

value._uniqueId: GOOGLESHEETS_SPREADSHEETS_VALUES_BATCH_GET_BY_DATA_FILTER_Batch Get Spreadsheet Values by Data Filter_41

dataFilters.description: Required. An array of data filter objects used to match ranges of values to retrieve. Each filter can specify either 'a1Range' (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:B5') or 'gridRange'. Must be provided as a list, e.g., [{'a1Range': 'Sheet1!A1:B5'}]. A single filter object will be automatically wrapped in a list. This parameter is required.
dataFilters.title: Datafilters

dateTimeRenderOption.description: How dates, times, and durations should be represented in the output. This is ignored if valueRenderOption is FORMATTED_VALUE. The default dateTime render option is SERIAL_NUMBER. Please provide a value of type string.
dateTimeRenderOption.title: Datetimerenderoption

majorDimension.description: The major dimension that results should use. For example, if the spreadsheet data is: A1=1,B1=2,A2=3,B2=4, then a request that selects that range and sets majorDimension=ROWS returns [[1,2],[3,4]], whereas a request that sets majorDimension=COLUMNS returns [[1,3],[2,4]]. Please provide a value of type string.
majorDimension.title: Majordimension

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve data from. This is the unique identifier found in the spreadsheet URL (e.g., in 'https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/SPREADSHEET_ID/edit', the ID is the SPREADSHEET_ID part). Typical Google Sheets IDs are approximately 44 characters long and contain alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheetid

valueRenderOption.description: How values should be represented in the output. The default render option is FORMATTED_VALUE. Please provide a value of type string.
valueRenderOption.title: Valuerenderoption

value.description: Tool to return one or more ranges of values from a spreadsheet that match the specified data filters. Use when you need to retrieve specific data sets based on filtering criteria rather than entire sheets or fixed ranges.
value.display_description: Tool to return one or more ranges of values from a spreadsheet that match the specified data filters. Use when you need to retrieve specific data sets based on filtering criteria rather than entire sheets or fixed ranges.
value.display_name: Batch Get Spreadsheet Values by Data Filter
value.name: googlesheets_spreadsheets_values_batch_get_by_

chart_id.description: The unique numeric identifier of the chart to update within the spreadsheet. This is the chartId value returned when the chart was created or can be retrieved using the 'Get Spreadsheet Info' action (look for sheets[].charts[].chartId in the response). The chart must already exist in the specified spreadsheet. Please provide a value of type integer. This parameter is required.
chart_id.title: Chart Id

chart_spec.description: The complete ChartSpec object defining the updated chart specification. This replaces the chart's existing specification but does not move or resize the chart. Must set exactly one chart type field: basicChart, pieChart, bubbleChart, candlestickChart, histogramChart, treemapChart, waterfallChart, orgChart, or scorecardChart. To change chart position or size, use a separate UpdateEmbeddedObjectPosition request. See https://developers.google.com/sheets/api/reference/rest/v4/spreadsheets/charts#ChartSpec. This parameter is required.
chart_spec.title: Chart Spec

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Sheets spreadsheet containing the chart to update. Must be the actual spreadsheet ID from the URL (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'), NOT the spreadsheet name or title. Find it in the URL: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/SPREADSHEET_ID/edit. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

value.description: Update the specification of an existing chart in a Google Sheets spreadsheet. Use this action when you need to modify an existing chart's properties such as its title, chart type, data ranges, colors, axes, or other visual settings. This action updates the chart's specification but does not change its position or size on the sheet.
value.display_description: Update the specification of an existing chart in a Google Sheets spreadsheet. Use this action when you need to modify an existing chart's properties such as its title, chart type, data ranges, colors, axes, or other visual settings. This action updates the chart's specification but does not change its position or size on the sheet.
value.name: googlesheets_update_chart

dimension.description: Whether to update rows or columns. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
dimension.title: Dimension

end_index.description: The zero-based end index (exclusive) of the dimension range to update. For example, to update rows 5-9, use start_index=5 and end_index=10. Please provide a value of type integer. This parameter is required.
end_index.title: End Index

hidden_by_user.description: Whether to hide (true) or unhide (false) the specified rows/columns. At least one of hidden_by_user or pixel_size must be provided. Please provide a value of type boolean.
hidden_by_user.title: Hidden By User

include_spreadsheet_in_response.description: Whether to include the updated spreadsheet in the response. Please provide a value of type boolean.
include_spreadsheet_in_response.title: Include Spreadsheet In Response

pixel_size.description: The height (for rows) or width (for columns) in pixels. Must be a positive integer. At least one of hidden_by_user or pixel_size must be provided. Please provide a value of type integer.
pixel_size.title: Pixel Size

response_include_grid_data.description: Whether to include grid data in the response (only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true). Please provide a value of type boolean.
response_include_grid_data.title: Response Include Grid Data

response_ranges.description: Limits the ranges included in the response spreadsheet (only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true).
response_ranges.title: Response Ranges

sheet_id.description: The numeric ID of the sheet (tab). Either sheet_id or sheet_name must be provided. If both are provided, sheet_name will be resolved to sheet_id and override this value. Please provide a value of type integer.
sheet_id.title: Sheet Id

sheet_name.description: The name of the sheet (tab). If provided, this will be resolved to the numeric sheet_id using GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO. Either sheet_id or sheet_name must be provided. Please provide a value of type string.
sheet_name.title: Sheet Name

spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

start_index.description: The zero-based start index (inclusive) of the dimension range to update. For rows, 0 is the first row; for columns, 0 is column A. Please provide a value of type integer. This parameter is required.
start_index.title: Start Index

value.description: Tool to hide/unhide rows or columns and set row heights or column widths. Use when you need to change visibility or pixel sizing of dimensions in a Google Sheet.
value.display_description: Tool to hide/unhide rows or columns and set row heights or column widths. Use when you need to change visibility or pixel sizing of dimensions in a Google Sheet.
value.name: googlesheets_update_dimension_properties

includeSpreadsheetInResponse.description: Determines if the update response should include the spreadsheet resource. When true, the response will include the full updated spreadsheet. Please provide a value of type boolean.
includeSpreadsheetInResponse.title: Includespreadsheetinresponse

responseIncludeGridData.description: True if grid data should be returned. Meaningful only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. When true, the response will include cell data for the specified ranges. Please provide a value of type boolean.
responseIncludeGridData.title: Responseincludegriddata

responseRanges.description: Limits the ranges included in the response spreadsheet. Meaningful only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. Ranges should be in A1 notation (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:B2').
responseRanges.title: Responseranges

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet containing the sheet to update. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheetid

updateSheetProperties.description: The details of the sheet properties to update. This parameter is required.

value.description: Tool to update properties of a sheet (worksheet) within a Google Spreadsheet, such as its title, index, visibility, tab color, or grid properties. Use this when you need to modify the metadata or appearance of a specific sheet.
value.display_description: Tool to update properties of a sheet (worksheet) within a Google Spreadsheet, such as its title, index, visibility, tab color, or grid properties. Use this when you need to modify the metadata or appearance of a specific sheet.
value.name: googlesheets_update_sheet_properties

fields.description: Field mask specifying which properties to update (comma-separated for multiple fields). Supports nested paths using dot notation (e.g., 'iterativeCalculationSettings.maxIterations') per Protocol Buffers FieldMask specification. The root 'properties' is implied and must not be included. Special case: When updating 'spreadsheetTheme', use the field mask 'spreadsheetTheme' (not nested paths like 'spreadsheetTheme.primaryFontFamily') and provide the complete theme object with all required fields. Wildcard '*' updates all properties. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
fields.title: Fields

includeSpreadsheetInResponse.description: Determines if the update response should include the full spreadsheet resource. When true, the response will include the entire updated spreadsheet with all sheets, properties, and metadata. Please provide a value of type boolean.
includeSpreadsheetInResponse.title: Includespreadsheetinresponse

properties.description: The spreadsheet-level properties to update (e.g., title, locale, timeZone, autoRecalc). At least one field within properties must be set. NOTE: To update individual sheet/tab properties (like renaming a specific sheet), use GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_SHEET_PROPERTIES instead. This parameter is required.

responseIncludeGridData.description: Determines if grid data (cell values) should be included in the response. Only meaningful if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. When true, the response will include cell data for the specified ranges or entire spreadsheet. Please provide a value of type boolean.
responseIncludeGridData.title: Responseincludegriddata

responseRanges.description: Limits the ranges included in the response spreadsheet. Only meaningful if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. Ranges should be in A1 notation (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:B2').
responseRanges.title: Responseranges

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheetid

value.description: Tool to update SPREADSHEET-LEVEL properties such as the spreadsheet's title, locale, time zone, or auto-recalculation settings. Use when you need to modify the overall configuration of a Google Spreadsheet. NOTE: To update individual SHEET properties (like renaming a specific sheet/tab), use GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_SHEET_PROPERTIES instead.
value.display_description: Tool to update SPREADSHEET-LEVEL properties such as the spreadsheet's title, locale, time zone, or auto-recalculation settings. Use when you need to modify the overall configuration of a Google Spreadsheet. NOTE: To update individual SHEET properties (like renaming a specific sheet/tab), use GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_SHEET_PROPERTIES instead.
value.name: googlesheets_update_spreadsheet_properties

data.description: The new values to apply to the spreadsheet. Each ValueRange specifies a range and the values to write to that range. Multiple ranges can be updated in a single request. This parameter is required.
data.title: Data

includeValuesInResponse.description: Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were updated. By default, responses do not include the updated values. Please provide a value of type boolean.
includeValuesInResponse.title: Includevaluesinresponse

responseDateTimeRenderOption.description: Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered. Only used if includeValuesInResponse is true. SERIAL_NUMBER (default): Dates are returned as numbers. FORMATTED_STRING: Dates are returned as formatted strings. Please provide a value of type string.
responseDateTimeRenderOption.title: Responsedatetimerenderoption

responseValueRenderOption.description: Determines how values in the response should be rendered. Only used if includeValuesInResponse is true. FORMATTED_VALUE (default): Values are formatted as displayed in the UI. UNFORMATTED_VALUE: Values are unformatted. FORMULA: Formulas are not evaluated. Please provide a value of type string.
responseValueRenderOption.title: Responsevaluerenderoption

spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update. This ID can be found in the URL of the spreadsheet (e.g., https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/{spreadsheet_id}/edit). Must be a valid Google Sheets spreadsheet ID. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

valueInputOption.description: How the input data should be interpreted. RAW: Values are stored exactly as entered, without parsing. USER_ENTERED: Values are parsed as if typed by a user (numbers stay numbers, strings prefixed with '=' become formulas, etc.). INPUT_VALUE_OPTION_UNSPECIFIED: Default input value option is not specified. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
valueInputOption.title: Valueinputoption

value.description: Tool to set values in one or more ranges of a spreadsheet. Use when you need to update multiple ranges in a single operation for better performance.
value.display_description: Tool to set values in one or more ranges of a spreadsheet. Use when you need to update multiple ranges in a single operation for better performance.
value.name: googlesheets_update_values_batch

headers.description: List of column names for the data. These will be matched against sheet headers. If a column doesn't exist in the sheet, it will be added automatically. Order doesn't need to match sheet order. Can be auto-derived from the first row in 'rows' if not provided. Example inputs: ['Email', 'Phone', 'Status'] for contact updates, ['Lead ID', 'Name', 'Score'] for CRM, ['SKU', 'Stock', 'Price'] for inventory.
headers.title: Headers

keyColumn.description: The column NAME (header text) to use as unique identifier for matching rows. Must be an actual header name from the sheet (e.g., 'Email', 'Lead ID', 'SKU'), NOT a column letter (e.g., 'A', 'B', 'C'). If you provide a column letter like 'A', it will be automatically converted to the header name at that column position. If neither 'key_column' nor 'key_column_index' is provided, defaults to the first column (index 0). Please provide a value of type string.
keyColumn.title: Keycolumn

key_column_index.description: The 0-based column index to use as unique identifier for matching rows. Alternative to 'key_column' - will be converted to column name using headers. If neither 'key_column' nor 'key_column_index' is provided, defaults to 0 (first column). Example: 0 for first column, 1 for second column.
key_column_index.title: Key Column Index

normalization_message.description: Internal field to track input normalization (e.g., row truncation). Not part of API. Please provide a value of type string.
normalization_message.title: Normalization Message

rows.description: 2D array of data rows to upsert. IMPORTANT: If 'headers' is NOT provided, the FIRST row is treated as column headers and remaining rows as data - so you need at least 2 rows (1 header + 1 data). If 'headers' IS provided separately, then ALL rows in this array are treated as data rows. Each row should have the same number of values as headers. If a row has MORE values than headers: with strict_mode=true (default), an error is returned showing which rows are affected; with strict_mode=false, extra values are silently truncated. If a row has FEWER values than headers, an error is returned during execution. Cell values can be strings, numbers, booleans, or null. Example with headers provided: headers=['Email','Status'], rows=[['john@ex.com','Active']] (1 data row). Example without headers: rows=[['Email','Status'],['john@ex.com','Active']] (row 1 = headers, row 2 = data). This parameter is required.
rows.title: Rows

sheetName.description: The name of the sheet/tab within the spreadsheet. Note: Google Sheets creates default sheets with localized names based on account language (e.g., 'Sheet1' for English, '工作表1' for Chinese, 'Hoja1' for Spanish, 'Feuille1' for French, 'Planilha1' for Portuguese, 'Лист1' for Russian). If you specify a common default name and the sheet is not found, the action will automatically use the first sheet if only one exists. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
sheetName.title: Sheetname

spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet. Must be a non-empty string, typically a 44-character alphanumeric string found in the spreadsheet URL. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheetid

strictMode.description: Controls how rows with mismatched column counts are handled. When True (default), an error is returned if any row has more values than headers - the error message shows exactly which rows are affected and what values would need to be removed. When False, extra values are silently truncated to match the header count. Set to False only if you explicitly want automatic truncation of extra values. Please provide a value of type boolean.
strictMode.title: Strictmode

tableStart.description: Cell where the table starts (where headers are located). Defaults to 'A1'. Use this if your table is offset (e.g., 'C5', 'D10'). Please provide a value of type string.
tableStart.title: Tablestart

value.description: Upsert rows - update existing rows by key, append new ones. Automatically handles column mapping and partial updates. Use for: CRM syncs (match Lead ID), transaction imports (match Transaction ID), inventory updates (match SKU), calendar syncs (match Event ID). Features: - Auto-adds missing columns to sheet - Partial column updates (only update Phone + Status, preserve other columns) - Column order doesn't matter (auto-maps by header name) - Prevents duplicates by matching key column Example inputs: - Contact update: keyColumn='Email', headers=['Email','Phone','Status'], data=[['john@ex.com','555-0101','Active']] - Inventory sync: keyColumn='SKU', headers=['SKU','Stock','Price'], data=[['WIDGET-001',50,9.99],['GADGET-002',30,19.99]] - CRM lead update: keyColumn='Lead ID', headers=['Lead ID','Score','Status'], data=[['L-12345',85,'Hot']] - Partial update: keyColumn='Email', headers=['Email','Phone'] (only updates Phone, preserves Name/Address/etc)
value.display_description: Upsert rows - update existing rows by key, append new ones. Automatically handles column mapping and partial updates. Use for: CRM syncs (match Lead ID), transaction imports (match Transaction ID), inventory updates (match SKU), calendar syncs (match Event ID). Features: - Auto-adds missing columns to sheet - Partial column updates (only update Phone + Status, preserve other columns) - Column order doesn't matter (auto-maps by header name) - Prevents duplicates by matching key column Example inputs: - Contact update: keyColumn='Email', headers=['Email','Phone','Status'], data=[['john@ex.com','555-0101','Active']] - Inventory sync: keyColumn='SKU', headers=['SKU','Stock','Price'], data=[['WIDGET-001',50,9.99],['GADGET-002',30,19.99]] - CRM lead update: keyColumn='Lead ID', headers=['Lead ID','Score','Status'], data=[['L-12345',85,'Hot']] - Partial update: keyColumn='Email', headers=['Email','Phone'] (only updates Phone, preserves Name/Address/etc)
value.name: googlesheets_upsert_rows

date_time_render_option.description: How dates, times, and durations should be represented in the output. SERIAL_NUMBER: Dates are returned as serial numbers (default). FORMATTED_STRING: Dates returned as formatted strings. Please provide a value of type string.
date_time_render_option.title: Date Time Render Option

end_row.description: 1-based row number to stop reading at (inclusive). Use with start_row for pagination to avoid large response errors. Example: start_row=501, end_row=1000 fetches rows 501-1000. Please provide a value of type integer.
end_row.title: End Row

major_dimension.description: The major dimension for results. Please provide a value of type string.
major_dimension.title: Major Dimension

range.description: The A1 notation or R1C1 notation of the range to retrieve values from. If the sheet name contains spaces or special characters, wrap the sheet name in single quotes (e.g., "'My Sheet'!A1:B2"). Without single quotes, the API will return a 400 error for sheet names with spaces. Examples: 'Sheet1!A1:B3', "'Sheet With Spaces'!A1:D5", 'A1:D5' (no sheet name uses first visible sheet), 'Sheet1!A:A' (entire column), 'SheetName' (entire sheet). Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
range.title: Range

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet from which to retrieve values. This is the long alphanumeric string found in the spreadsheet URL between '/d/' and '/edit' (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'). WARNING: Do NOT use the spreadsheet name or title (e.g., 'My Sales Report'); you must use the actual ID from the URL. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

start_row.description: 1-based row number to start reading from (inclusive). Use with end_row for pagination to avoid large response errors. Example: start_row=1, end_row=500 fetches the first 500 rows. Please provide a value of type integer.
start_row.title: Start Row

value_render_option.description: How values should be rendered in the output. FORMATTED_VALUE: Values are calculated and formatted (default). UNFORMATTED_VALUE: Values are calculated but not formatted. FORMULA: Values are not calculated; the formula is returned instead. Please provide a value of type string.
value_render_option.title: Value Render Option

value.description: Returns a range of values from a spreadsheet. Use when you need to read data from specific cells or ranges in a Google Sheet.
value.display_description: Returns a range of values from a spreadsheet. Use when you need to read data from specific cells or ranges in a Google Sheet.
value.name: googlesheets_values_get

auto_expand_sheet.description: If True (default), automatically expands the sheet's dimensions (adds columns/rows) when the target range exceeds the current grid limits. If False, the operation will fail with an error if the range exceeds grid limits. Please provide a value of type boolean.
auto_expand_sheet.title: Auto Expand Sheet

include_values_in_response.description: Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were updated. By default, responses do not include the updated values. Please provide a value of type boolean.
include_values_in_response.title: Include Values In Response

major_dimension.description: The major dimension of the values. ROWS (default) means each inner array is a row of values. COLUMNS means each inner array is a column of values. Defaults to ROWS if unspecified. Please provide a value of type string.
major_dimension.title: Major Dimension

range.description: The A1 notation of the range to update values in (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:C2', 'MySheet!C:C', or 'A1:D5'). Must be actual cell references, not placeholder values. If the sheet name is omitted (e.g., 'A1:B2'), the operation applies to the first visible sheet. IMPORTANT: The range must not exceed the sheet's grid dimensions. By default, new sheets have 1000 rows and 26 columns (A-Z). If you need to write to columns beyond Z (e.g., AA, AB), first expand the sheet using GOOGLESHEETS_APPEND_DIMENSION or check the current dimensions using GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
range.title: Range

response_datetime_render_option.description: Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered. Only used if includeValuesInResponse is true. SERIAL_NUMBER (default): Dates are returned as numbers. FORMATTED_STRING: Dates are returned as strings formatted per the cell's locale. Please provide a value of type string.
response_datetime_render_option.title: Response Datetime Render Option

response_value_render_option.description: Determines how values in the response should be rendered. Only used if includeValuesInResponse is true. FORMATTED_VALUE (default): Values are formatted as displayed in the UI. UNFORMATTED_VALUE: Values are unformatted (numbers, booleans, formulas). FORMULA: Formulas are not evaluated and remain as text. Please provide a value of type string.
response_value_render_option.title: Response Value Render Option

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet to update. This ID can be found in the URL of the spreadsheet (e.g., https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/{spreadsheet_id}/edit). Must be a non-empty string. Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

value_input_option.description: How the input data should be interpreted. RAW: Values are stored exactly as entered, without parsing (dates, formulas, etc. remain as strings). USER_ENTERED: Values are parsed as if typed by a user (numbers stay numbers, strings prefixed with '=' become formulas, etc.). Please provide a value of type string. This parameter is required.
value_input_option.title: Value Input Option

values.description: The data to write. This is an array of arrays, the outer array representing all the data and each inner array representing a major dimension. Each item in the inner array corresponds with one cell. This parameter is required.
values.title: Values

value.description: Tool to set values in a range of a Google Spreadsheet. Use when you need to update or overwrite existing cell values in a specific range.
value.display_description: Tool to set values in a range of a Google Spreadsheet. Use when you need to update or overwrite existing cell values in a specific range.
value.name: googlesheets_values_update
